problem_statement: The blind-box item library should not model image anchors because the tool cannot inspect images. The fourth content category should therefore be a default-safe scene enrichment pool rather than conditional, anchor-required, or risky content.

recommended_direction.summary: Refactor the content model into four default-safe pools: core_items, support_items, visible_small_items, and scene_expansion_items. Remove blocked_or_risky from content categories and enforce risks through blocked_patterns tests.

four_pool_model.core_items: Most typical and stable primary objects for the scene.
four_pool_model.support_items: Tools, containers, storage, carriers, or functional accessories around core objects.
four_pool_model.visible_small_items: Grouped, blocky, carrier-backed small items with clear boundaries.
four_pool_model.scene_expansion_items: Medium-or-larger scene enrichment objects that do not require image anchors and add variety beyond core/support items.

recommended_direction.risk_handling: Use blocked_patterns / blocked_terms in tests and validation, not item pools.

short_term_patch: Remove blocked_or_risky from pilot content bundles.
Add blocked_patterns / blocked_terms as validation constants or test fixtures.
Update legacy four-column mapping so large/medium/small remain populated from safe pools and hanging is not force-filled with low-quality thin objects.
Rewrite the three pilot boxes 15/16/17 using the four-pool model.
quality_gates: scene_expansion_items must not require image facts such as visible monitor, whiteboard, umbrella pole, wall hook, or available side space.
scene_expansion_items must be medium-or-larger, common, boundary-clear, independently circleable, and scene-relevant.
visible_small_items must be grouped, blocky, or carrier-backed.
No default pool may contain thin strings, tassels, edge lines, scratches, folds, bubbles, shadows, highlights, transparent/reflective/glowing objects, animal bodies, or tiny trace-like objects.
blocked_or_risky must not exist as a candidate item pool in the pilot model.
open_questions: Should hanging output be reduced/skipped when no high-quality safe hanging candidate exists?
Should scene_expansion_items be sampled into large only, or large plus medium with lower weight?
Should blocked_patterns live in data/blind_boxes.py or in the test module only?
recommended_next_step: Run spec-generator on the updated handoff-spec.json, then plan and execute the four-pool content refactor.
